title: APIv2 service dumps core
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**
When force deleting running container through the REST API (using cockpit), sometimes podman coredumps. I don't have exact reproducer as it seems it is happening somewhat randomly.

**Describe the results you received:**

```
Jun 17 14:55:01 zahlmaschine systemd-coredump[231935]: Process 231044 (podman) of user 0 dumped core.
                                                       
                                                       Stack trace of thread 231866:
                                                       #0  0x00005570462f89b1 runtime.raise (podman + 0x9b39b1)
                                                       #1  0x00005570462dcc5e runtime.sigfwdgo (podman + 0x997c5e)
                                                       #2  0x00005570462db594 runtime.sigtrampgo (podman + 0x996594)
                                                       #3  0x00005570462f8d23 runtime.sigtramp (podman + 0x9b3d23)
                                                       #4  0x00007f2d7f036a90 __restore_rt (libpthread.so.0 + 0x14a90)
                                                       #5  0x00005570462f89b1 runtime.raise (podman + 0x9b39b1)
                                                       #6  0x00005570462dc82a runtime.crash (podman + 0x99782a)
                                                       #7  0x00005570462c5966 runtime.fatalpanic (podman + 0x980966)
                                                       #8  0x00005570462c5301 runtime.gopanic (podman + 0x980301)
                                                       #9  0x0000557046297033 runtime.closechan (podman + 0x952033)
                                                       #10 0x00005570462f502d runtime.call32 (podman + 0x9b002d)
                                                       #11 0x00005570462c4dea runtime.reflectcallSave (podman + 0x97fdea)
                                                       #12 0x00005570462c4cab runtime.runOpenDeferFrame (podman + 0x97fcab)
                                                       #13 0x00005570462c4ffa runtime.gopanic (podman + 0x97fffa)
                                                       #14 0x0000557046296b59 runtime.chansend (podman + 0x951b59)
                                                       #15 0x0000557046296575 runtime.chansend1 (podman + 0x951575)
                                                       #16 0x0000557046cfc76c github.com/containers/libpod/libpod/events.EventLogFile.Read (podman + 0x13b776c)
                                                       #17 0x0000557046cfd8c2 github.com/containers/libpod/libpod/events.(*EventLogFile).Read (podman + 0x13b88c2)
                                                       #18 0x00005570471fd48e github.com/containers/libpod/libpod.(*Runtime).Events (podman + 0x18b848e)
                                                       #19 0x00005570474d8176 github.com/containers/libpod/pkg/api/handlers/compat.GetEvents.func1 (podman + 0x1b93176)
                                                       #20 0x00005570462f6dd1 runtime.goexit (podman + 0x9b1dd1)
```

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
$ podman version
Version:      2.0.0-rc6
API Version:  1
Go Version:   go1.14.3
Built:        Thu Jan  1 01:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman-2.0.0-0.2.rc6.fc32.x86_64
```
Core, it is `lz4`, just had to add `.txt` at the end so I could upload it to github.
[core.podman.0.4bd1138493f74a88aa50d002cf75109a.231044.1592398500000000000000.lz4.txt](https://github.com/containers/libpod/files/4792626/core.podman.0.4bd1138493f74a88aa50d002cf75109a.231044.1592398500000000000000.lz4.txt)

comments.body:
based on the core file, the problem is sending on a closed channel. still investigating on the closed channel and why that is happening.  note this is not in the rm code, it seems it is when we are monitoring the events.

```
#19 0x00005570474d8176 github.com/containers/libpod/pkg/api/handlers/compat.GetEvents.func1 (podman + 0x1b93176)
```
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

I think this still happens. Just the other day while working on c-podman tests I saw podman coredumping due to sending events into closed channel. (I created container and then logged out of cockpit, which closed the channel but podman still sent event about container exiting).
this should have been all cleaned up now. i believe @jwhonce got them all now.
